text:
Once upon a time, in a bustling forest, there lived a mischievous squirrel named Sammy. Sammy was known for his grand plan of stealing sunflower seeds from Mr. Hawthorne's amazing sunflower garden. He'd weave through the branches, find the ripest treasures, and stash them away in his cheekiest caches.

One sunny morning, Sammy decided his antics would draw more attention. So, he pretended to be attacked by a scary owl and hid behind a large pine.

As Mr. Hawthorne came by singing his cheerful songs, Sammy peeked through the branches. He saw Mr. Hawthorne plucking beans from another plant that bloomed just like sunflowers.

"Look!" Sammy cheered, thinking his plan would succeed.

But Mr. Hawthorne didn't notice him. Instead, he said to his wife, "I think this plant could use some care. Shall we plant something lovely?"

The women nodded and gently uprooted the beanflower. Sammy scurried away as
